workflows.name: platform-role-governance
workflows.trigger: Any Bears platform part, auth, gateway, reusable surface, or platform subagent routing decision
input_packet: target path or concrete platform part
current Spec Kit artifact snapshot when available
security_rules: do not expose secrets or production data
supporting security role attaches for trust-boundary changes

workflows.name: workflow-router
workflows.trigger: Any Bears Telegram task or routing decision
input_packet: operator intent: formatting, migration, plugin skill, runtime implementation, or validation
security_rules: canonical role gate before Telegram skill-bundle validators
no live Telegram mutation from routing

workflows.name: aiogram-migration
workflows.trigger: Telegram bot framework rewrite or Aiogram architecture work
security_rules: no runtime rewrite before readiness gate opens
no live webhook/polling mutation without approval

workflows.name: telegram-quality-testing
workflows.trigger: Messages, keyboards, callbacks, command menus, FSM or approval loops
security_rules: no private chat logs or token values

workflows.name: telegram-skill-factory
workflows.trigger: Create or update a Telegram workflow skill inside plugins/bears
security_rules: no broad duplicate skills without routing rule
no connector/runtime/live Telegram claim without schema support
No raw secrets, bot tokens, private chat payloads, or production data in docs/tests/logs.
Live Telegram mutations require explicit operator approval after read-only evidence.
Telegram approvals use normal preflight, audit, and rollback rules.
workspace_rules: Specialist platform role is required before auth/gateway/platform-part implementation; missing coverage returns ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER.
Workspace-map is operator-disabled; use local docs and targeted evidence.
Narrow local project instructions override central plugin guidance for implementation details.
Aiogram migration backlog is mandatory before bot runtime rewrites; use scripts/telegram_migration_backlog.py validate.

surfaces.name: codex-telegram
surfaces.target_state: production Telegram MCP endpoint with workspace-control registration only
surfaces.test_status: product pytest plus MCP dry-run smoke required before code work
surfaces.next_action: Keep product MCP code under dev/app/codex-telegram, register only the production Kubernetes MCP endpoint from workspace-control, and do not create a dev/workspace adapter.

surfaces.name: vpnbot
surfaces.target_state: consume telegram-platform-core primitives through a thin product adapter
surfaces.test_status: migration backlog validation only; product tests required before code work
surfaces.next_action: Separate reusable Telegram primitives from VPN-only product behavior.; Define callback, RBAC, idempotency, and rendering tests for adapter seams.

surfaces.name: seller-telegram-notifications
surfaces.target_state: non-bot Telegram surface; keep adapter or exception path instead of bot runtime rewrite

surfaces.name: metamask-telegram-bot
surfaces.current_framework_status: telegraf / file-level source evidence only; package version not verified in this governance pass
surfaces.target_state: inventory-only rewrite candidate; keep implementation blocked until owner artifacts and role mapping exist
surfaces.test_status: inventory governance only; product tests and behavior inventory required before code work
surfaces.next_action: Keep as inventory-only Telegram bot candidate until owning target artifacts, role mapping, and behavior tests exist.; Do not start framework rewrite from file-level evidence alone.

surfaces.name: emu-ants-telegram-bridge
surfaces.current_framework_status: raw Telegram Bot API bridge / file-level source evidence only
surfaces.target_state: non-bot Telegram bridge; keep exception/adapter path unless future product scope proves bot-runtime behavior
surfaces.test_status: inventory governance only; owner/artifact evidence required before code work
surfaces.exception_status: Non-bot bridge candidate from file-level evidence; no forced Aiogram rewrite.
surfaces.next_action: Keep as inventory-only non-bot bridge candidate until ownership, artifacts, and bridge boundary documentation exist.; Do not infer implementation readiness from direct Bot API calls alone.

surfaces.name: vpn-telegram-msp-notify
surfaces.current_framework_status: raw Telegram Bot API notifier / file-level source evidence only
surfaces.target_state: non-bot notification bridge; keep adapter path unless promoted to interactive bot runtime
surfaces.test_status: inventory governance only; owner/artifact evidence required before code work
surfaces.exception_status: Non-bot notification bridge from sanitized-alert notifier evidence; no forced Aiogram rewrite.
surfaces.next_action: Keep as inventory-only notification bridge candidate until owner artifacts and boundary tests exist.; Do not convert alert delivery helpers into bot-runtime backlog without stronger product evidence.

surfaces.name: tgsearch
surfaces.current_framework_status: telethon / verified by local source scan, exact version not checked in this backlog pass
surfaces.target_state: non-bot Telegram surface; keep adapter or exception path instead of bot runtime rewrite
surfaces.test_status: migration backlog validation only; product tests required before code work
surfaces.exception_status: Collector exception record required before any forced bot-runtime abstraction.
surfaces.next_action: Keep as read-only collector adapter; do not force Aiogram bot callback abstractions.; Document session hygiene and read-only evidence boundaries if this surface joins the shared platform.

surfaces.name: theants
surfaces.current_framework_status: custom Bot API polling client / not-aiogram / legacy sources preserved at projects/theants and dev/products/theants
surfaces.target_state: app directory under canonical BearsCLOUD/apps with adapter seam and tests before any Aiogram 3 runtime rewrite
surfaces.test_status: dev-core split proof plus product tests required before code work
surfaces.next_action: Preserve legacy sources as migration/archive evidence, route canonical product work through BearsCLOUD/apps and bears-product-app-zone-engineer, and use Telegram platform review only for adapter-pattern changes after tests.

surfaces.name: proxybot
surfaces.target_state: defer until source is recovered and ownership is verified
surfaces.test_status: migration backlog validation only; product tests required before code work
surfaces.exception_status: Direct source recovery or canonical repository link required before migration planning.
surfaces.next_action: Locate canonical source or remove the placeholder from active migration scope.; Do not infer current contracts from historical mentions.
